violations.text: The dishwasher failed to wash his/her hands between loading soiled and unloading clean dishes from the dishwasher. Food employees must clean their hands after handling soiled equipment or utensils. PIC and staff informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2026

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (scallions) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Scallions disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2026

Bananas and a lock were observed in the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Items were removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2026

Multiple foods were observed held at 45-50 degrees Fahrenheit in a prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods that were placed in unit today were moved to other units. All food requiring refrigeration that were left in unit overnight were disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2026
ROP salmon and tuna were observed fully thawed with packaging intact. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2026

Working containers of sauces, oils, spices are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2026

Table utensils (forks, knives, spoons) in the dish machine utensil tray are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2026
Water was not available in the handwashing sink in the kitchen. PIC stated service was recently performed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2026
The establishment does not employ a Rhode Island Food Protection Manager. Establishment to register Rhode Island Certified Food Protection Manager. New Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by tools and mop . A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as pita bread) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

The door to the outside, located by restrooms, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2024
